Today we are going to be looking at Miniax 2.7. Miniax's self-improving AI agent to automate anything you want. So Miniax have just dropped their 2.7 model and it's a feature that no other AI company has shipped yet. It can look at its own mistakes, figure out what went wrong and fix itself. Not you fixing it, not you reprompting it five times. The AI fixes itself. Now let me say that again because it actually matters. This AI agent looks at what it built, sees the errors, rewrites the code, tries again on its own till it works. That means, you know, if you're sitting there thinking, I'm not technical enough to use AI agents, that excuse just got deleted because the AI is now doing the debugging for you. So, today I'm going to show you exactly how Miniax 2.7 works, how this AI super agent works, what makes it different from every model out there, and why the self-improving ability changes the game for anyone who wants to automate their business. So, first of all, let's talk about this, right? What is Miniax M2.7 and why should you care? So let's start from scratch. Miniax is a Chinese AI company. They build large language models kind of like open II built chat cheap or Google builds Gemini. But here's where it gets interesting. Minia is their latest model and it introduced something called self-improving capabilities. Now what does that actually mean in plain English? It means when you give Minia a task like build me a landing page or write me an automation and it makes a mistake. It doesn't just stop and say sorry I can't do that mate. It looks at the error, figures out what went wrong, rewrites his own work, tries again automatically without you doing anything. Right? So, think of it like having an employee who makes a mistake on a report, catches it themselves before you even see it, fixes it, and hands you the corrected version. That's Miniax M2.7, and we're testing it out today. So, I want to show you something that I would call the feedback loop engine framework. Right now, most people use AI like a vending machine. They put in a prompt, they get an output. If you the output is bad, you try again manually. That's the old way. The feedback loop engine flips this completely because instead of you fixing the AI's work, the AI reviews its own work, identifies what's wrong, corrects it, and keeps looping until the output is right. And then here's the powerful part. It actually remembers what works. So next time it gets even better. This is self-improving as we all know, right?
